title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: RNAPeaks R package producing publication-quality visualizations RNA-binding protein (RBP) peaks overlaid annotated gene structures. Starting BED file protein binding sites, RNAPeaks draws peak rectangle corresponding gene model — showing exons, UTRs, introns accurate genomic scale. package provides four analysis modes:

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
code: # Bioconductor dependencies if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c(     "GenomicRanges", "IRanges", "S4Vectors", "BiocGenerics",     "GenomeInfoDb", "AnnotationHub", "Biostrings", "BSgenome" ))  # Install RNAPeaks from GitHub devtools::install_github("Krushna-B/RNAPeaks")

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
code: library(RNAPeaks)

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: Gene structure diagrams built Ensembl GTF annotation. Load per session LoadGTF() optionally cache disk avoid re-downloading subsequent sessions. Supported species: "Human" (Ensembl GRCh38, AH110867) "Mouse" (Ensembl GRCm38, AH47076).
code: # First call downloads from AnnotationHub (~1–2 min) gtf <- LoadGTF(species = "Human")  # Cache locally for instant loading next time saveRDS(gtf, "human_gtf.rds") # The package ships with a pre-loaded GTF — no download required data(gtf_human) gtf <- gtf_human

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: Use checkBed() validate normalize BED-format data frame passing plotting function. Columns mapped position: package includes ready--use sample dataset:
code: my_bed <- read.table("my_peaks.bed", header = FALSE, sep = "\t") my_bed <- checkBed(my_bed) head(sample_bed) #>   chr    start      end           tag score strand #> 1  21  8401778  8401840 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      + #> 2  19  4035770  4035869 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      + #> 3  11 93721690 93721719 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      + #> 4  19  2270224  2270300 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      + #> 5   m    12167    12208 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      + #> 6  16  2760062  2760143 AARS_K562_IDR  1000      +

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: PlotGene() plots RBP peaks overlap single gene interest. Key parameters:
code: result <- PlotGene(     bed    = sample_bed,     geneID = "GAPDH",     gtf    = gtf )  result$plot   # display the plot result$csv    # filtered peaks used in the figure

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: PlotRegion() displays genes overlapping peaks within specified genomic window.
code: result <- PlotRegion(     bed    = sample_bed,     gtf    = gtf,     Chr    = "12",     Start  = 56000000,     End    = 56050000,     Strand = "+" ) result$plot

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: createSplicingMap() quantifies RBP binding frequency every position across four-region window spanning exon-intron junctions skipped-exon (SE) events rMATS output. function classifies events three groups based IncLevelDifference PValue / FDR: Retained — significantly inclusion condition 1 Excluded — significantly skipping condition 1 Control — non-significant, stable inclusion bootstrap procedure samples matched number control events across control_iterations replicates build null distribution. Positions Retained Excluded frequencies exceed control z-score threshold highlighted significant regions.
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file  = sample_bed,     SEMATS    = sample_se.mats ) createSplicingMap(     bed_file           = sample_bed,     SEMATS             = sample_se.mats,     cores              = 4,           # parallel processing     control_iterations = 20,          # bootstrap replicates     show_significance  = TRUE,        # overlay significance bars     WidthIntoExon      = 50,          # bp into flanking exons     WidthIntoIntron    = 300          # bp into flanking introns )

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks
text: createSequenceMap() works identically createSplicingMap() instead protein binding, measures per-position frequency target sequence motif. Full IUPAC ambiguity codes supported. Common IUPAC codes:
code: library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)  # YCAY motif (Y = C or T; classic NOVA binding site) createSequenceMap(     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats,     sequence = "YCAY" )

title: Getting Started with RNAPeaks

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: article covers PlotGene() PlotRegion() depth — two functions producing peak--gene-structure diagrams.
code: library(RNAPeaks) data(gtf_human)

title: Gene-Level Visualization
code: result <- PlotGene(     bed    = sample_bed,     geneID = "GAPDH",     gtf    = gtf_human ) result$plot

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: default, RNAPeaks selects longest transcript. target specific isoform, supply Ensembl transcript ID:
code: result <- PlotGene(     bed    = sample_bed,     geneID = "TP53",     gtf    = gtf_human,     TxID   = "ENST00000269305" ) result$plot

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: order_by argument controls vertical stacking order RBP tracks:
code: # Order by peak count (most peaks at the bottom — default) PlotGene(bed = sample_bed, geneID = "GAPDH", gtf = gtf_human, order_by = "Count")  # Alphabetical order PlotGene(bed = sample_bed, geneID = "GAPDH", gtf = gtf_human, order_by = "Target")  # Explicit custom order PlotGene(     bed      = sample_bed,     geneID   = "GAPDH",     gtf      = gtf_human,     order_in = c("PROTEIN_A", "PROTEIN_B", "PROTEIN_C") )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: Use merge collapse peaks within given distance (bp) single rectangle, reducing visual noise fragmented binding sites:
code: PlotGene(     bed    = sample_bed,     geneID = "GAPDH",     gtf    = gtf_human,     merge  = 50     # merge peaks within 50 bp )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: negative-strand genes, x-axis runs right--left default (genomic coordinate order). Set five_to_three = TRUE flip axis 5′ always left:
code: PlotGene(     bed           = sample_bed,     geneID        = "ACTB",     gtf           = gtf_human,     five_to_three = TRUE )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: Draw shaded rectangle genomic interval call attention region interest:
code: PlotGene(     bed                       = sample_bed,     geneID                    = "GAPDH",     gtf                       = gtf_human,     highlighted_region_start  = 6643000,     highlighted_region_stop   = 6643500,     highlighted_region_color  = "gold",     highlighted_region_opacity = 0.4 )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: Add vertical dashed lines every exon-intron boundary:
code: PlotGene(     bed            = sample_bed,     geneID         = "GAPDH",     gtf            = gtf_human,     show_junctions = TRUE,     junction_color = "steelblue" )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: PlotRegion() renders genes (using longest transcript) whose features overlap specified genomic window, stacked vertically.

title: Gene-Level Visualization
code: result <- PlotRegion(     bed    = sample_bed,     gtf    = gtf_human,     Chr    = "12",     Start  = 56000000,     End    = 56050000,     Strand = "+" ) result$plot

what: Focusing on a single gene within a region
title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: Supply geneID include one gene still using specified coordinate window peak filtering:
code: PlotRegion(     bed    = sample_bed,     gtf    = gtf_human,     Chr    = "12",     Start  = 56000000,     End    = 56050000,     Strand = "+",     geneID = "GAPDH" )

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: PlotGene() PlotRegion() accept shared set styling arguments via .... commonly used listed .

title: Gene-Level Visualization
text: functions write PDF CSV default. Set paths explicitly pass NULL suppress file output:
code: result <- PlotGene(     bed                = sample_bed,     geneID             = "GAPDH",     gtf                = gtf_human,     RNA_Peaks_File_Path = "figures/GAPDH_peaks.pdf",     Bed_File_Path       = "data/GAPDH_peaks.csv" )  # Suppress file output — work with the ggplot object directly result <- PlotGene(     bed                = sample_bed,     geneID             = "GAPDH",     gtf                = gtf_human,     RNA_Peaks_File_Path = NULL,     Bed_File_Path       = NULL )  # Save manually with custom dimensions ggplot2::ggsave("GAPDH.pdf", result$plot, width = 18, height = 10)

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: createSplicingMap() createSequenceMap() quantify signal — protein binding sequence motif frequency — every position across four-region window spans exon-intron junctions skipped-exon (SE) alternative splicing events. functions accept SE.MATS output rMATS classify events three groups comparison: Retained — exon inclusion significantly increases condition 1 Excluded — exon skipping significantly increases condition 1 Control — events stable inclusion levels across conditions
code: library(RNAPeaks)

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: splicing event decomposed four equal-length windows (WidthIntoExon + WidthIntoIntron) positions: x-axis output plot shows positions within linearized window, vertical dashed lines region boundaries schematic skipped exon drawn beneath x-axis.
code: Upstream exon        Intron 1       Skipped exon      Intron 2     Downstream exon |──────|──────────────|──────|────────────────|──────|──────────────|──────|   UE    ←Region 1→    UI3    ←──Region 2──→   EX5    ←─Region 3─→   DI3    ←Region 4→        (UE-UI5)              (UI3-EX3)               (EX5-DI5)             (DI3-DE)

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file = sample_bed,     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: Control events fall group:
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file                        = sample_bed,     SEMATS                          = sample_se.mats,     p_valueRetainedAndExclusion     = 0.05,   # significance threshold for R/E     p_valueControls                 = 0.95,   # min p-value for controls     retained_IncLevelDifference     = 0.1,    # min ΔPSI for Retained     exclusion_IncLevelDifference    = -0.1,   # max ΔPSI for Excluded     Min_Count                       = 50,     # min junction read count     use_fdr                         = TRUE    # use FDR instead of raw p-value )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: Adjust far exons introns analysis extends:
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file        = sample_bed,     SEMATS          = sample_se.mats,     WidthIntoExon   = 75,    # 75 bp into each flanking exon     WidthIntoIntron = 400    # 400 bp into each flanking intron )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: moving average applied reduce positional noise. Adjust window size disable smoothing entirely:
code: # Wider smoothing window createSplicingMap(bed_file = sample_bed, SEMATS = sample_se.mats, moving_average = 100)  # No smoothing createSplicingMap(bed_file = sample_bed, SEMATS = sample_se.mats, moving_average = NULL)

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: large datasets, use multiple cores speed binding frequency calculation:
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file = sample_bed,     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats,     cores    = 4 )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: control distribution built bootstrapping: control_iterations replicates, random sample (n_retained + n_excluded) × control_multiplier control events drawn binding frequency computed. mean ± SD across replicates forms control ribbon. Significance assessed position using z-score: Contiguous runs ≥ min_consecutive significant positions reported enriched regions drawn colored bars plot.
code: z = (observed_frequency − control_mean) / control_SD createSplicingMap(     bed_file           = sample_bed,     SEMATS             = sample_se.mats,     control_multiplier = 2.0,   # sample_size = (R + E) * 2     control_iterations = 30,    # more iterations = tighter control band     z_threshold        = 1.96,  # p < 0.05 (used when use_fdr = FALSE)     min_consecutive    = 10,    # minimum run length to call a region     one_sided          = TRUE,  # test enrichment only (not depletion)     show_significance  = TRUE )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: z-score test assumes bootstrap replicates approximately normally distributed. Verify return_diagnostics = TRUE:
code: diag <- createSplicingMap(     bed_file           = sample_bed,     SEMATS             = sample_se.mats,     return_diagnostics = TRUE )  # Fraction of positions where bootstrap distribution passes Shapiro-Wilk mean(   apply(diag$bootstrap_matrix, 1, function(x) shapiro.test(x)$p.value) > 0.05 )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
code: freq_df <- createSplicingMap(     bed_file    = sample_bed,     SEMATS      = sample_se.mats,     return_data = TRUE ) head(freq_df)

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: createSequenceMap() accepts SE.MATS input parameters createSplicingMap(), instead protein binding sites measures per-position frequency target nucleotide motif genomic sequence.

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
code: library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)  createSequenceMap(     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats,     sequence = "YCAY"    # NOVA binding motif; Y = C or T )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: standard IUPAC ambiguity code supported:
code: # YGCY motif (MBNL binding site) createSequenceMap(SEMATS = sample_se.mats, sequence = "YGCY")  # Poly-C run createSequenceMap(SEMATS = sample_se.mats, sequence = "CCCC")

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: default createSequenceMap() uses BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38. Supply BSgenome object use different assembly:
code: library(BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10)  createSequenceMap(     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats,     sequence = "YCAY",     genome   = BSgenome.Mmusculus.UCSC.mm10 )

title: Splicing Maps and Sequence Motif Analysis
text: functions share common set visual parameters:

title: Authors and Citation
text: Krushna Bhanushali. Author, maintainer. Gilbert Giri. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bhanushali K, Giri G (2026). RNAPeaks: RNA-Binding Protein Peak Visualization Analysis. R package version 1.2.0, https://krushna-b.github.io/RNAPeaks/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {RNAPeaks: RNA-Binding Protein Peak Visualization and Analysis},   author = {Krushna Bhanushali and Gilbert Giri},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.2.0},   url = {https://krushna-b.github.io/RNAPeaks/}, }

title: RNAPeaks
text: Publication-quality visualization RNA-binding protein peaks gene structures  RNAPeaks R package visualizing RNA-binding protein (RBP) peaks overlaid annotated gene structures. accepts BED files protein binding sites Ensembl GTF annotations produce publication-ready figures showing RBPs bind relative exons, introns, UTRs. Splicing map sequence motif analyses quantify position-specific binding frequency around splice junctions bootstrap-based significance testing.

title: RNAPeaks
text: Full function reference articles available package website: https://krushna-b.github.io/RNAPeaks/

title: RNAPeaks
text: Additional capabilities: Bootstrap-based z-score significance testing enriched binding regions IUPAC ambiguity code support sequence motif search Multi-core parallel processing (cores parameter) large datasets Extensive ggplot2-based styling options Curated sample datasets immediate exploration

title: RNAPeaks
text: Install Bioconductor dependencies first:
code: if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c(     "GenomicRanges", "IRanges", "S4Vectors", "BiocGenerics",     "GenomeInfoDb", "AnnotationHub", "Biostrings", "BSgenome" ))

title: RNAPeaks
text: sequence motif analysis, also install reference genome:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("Krushna-B/RNAPeaks") BiocManager::install("BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38")

title: RNAPeaks
code: library(RNAPeaks)  # Load GTF annotation once per session (or load from a saved .rds file) gtf <- LoadGTF(species = "Human") saveRDS(gtf, "human_gtf.rds")   # cache for future sessions  # Plot RBP peaks on a single gene using the included sample data result <- PlotGene(     bed    = sample_bed,     geneID = "GAPDH",     gtf    = gtf )  result$plot   # ggplot2 object result$csv    # filtered BED data used in the plot

title: RNAPeaks
code: result <- PlotRegion(     bed    = sample_bed,     gtf    = gtf,     Chr    = "12",     Start  = 56000000,     End    = 56050000,     Strand = "+" ) result$plot

title: RNAPeaks
text: Quantify RBP binding frequency around exon-intron boundaries across skipped-exon splicing events: significance testing parallel processing:
code: createSplicingMap(     bed_file  = sample_bed,     SEMATS    = sample_se.mats ) createSplicingMap(     bed_file           = sample_bed,     SEMATS             = sample_se.mats,     cores              = 4,     control_iterations = 20,     show_significance  = TRUE )

title: RNAPeaks
text: Identify enrichment sequence motif (IUPAC codes supported) around splice junctions:
code: library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)  createSequenceMap(     SEMATS   = sample_se.mats,     sequence = "YCAY"           # Y = C or T )

text: createSplicingMap() createSequenceMap(), provide SE.MATS.JC.txt output rMATS. what: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
text: Loads GTF gene annotation data AnnotationHub Human Mouse. function can called load annotation, can passed functions like PlotGene() PlotRegion() avoid repeated downloads.

title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
code: LoadGTF(species = "Human")

title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
text: species Species load annotation : "Human" "Mouse".

title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
text: data frame containing GTF annotation columns including seqnames, start, end, strand, type, gene_id, gene_name, transcript_id, etc.

title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
text: Loading annotations AnnotationHub can take time first use. calling function separately, can: Load annotation reuse across multiple plots Save annotation disk faster future sessions Human annotations use Ensembl GTF (AH110867). Mouse annotations use Ensembl GTF (AH47076).

title: Loads GTF gene annotation data from AnnotationHub for Human or Mouse. This function can be called once to load the annotation, which can then be passed to other functions like PlotGene() or PlotRegion() to avoid repeated downloads. — LoadGTF
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load human GTF once   gtf <- LoadGTF(species = "Human")    # Optionally save for future sessions   saveRDS(gtf, "human_gtf.rds")    # Use in multiple plots without reloading   PlotGene(bed = bed, geneID = "TP53", gtf = gtf)   PlotGene(bed = bed, geneID = "BRCA1", gtf = gtf)    # Load from saved file in future sessions   gtf <- readRDS("human_gtf.rds") } # }

what: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
text: Creates publication-quality visualization RNA-binding protein peaks overlaid gene structure diagram showing exons, UTRs, introns.

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
code: PlotGene(   bed = NULL,   geneID = NULL,   gtf = NULL,   species = "Human",   TxID = NA,   Target_col = NULL,   omit = c(),   order_by = "Count",   order_in = NULL,   merge = 0,   peaks_width = 0.3,   utr_col = "dark gray",   peak_col = "purple",   exon_width = 0.5,   utr_width = 0.3,   exon_col = "black",   total_arrows = 6,   max_per_intron = 2,   five_to_three = FALSE,   RNA_Peaks_File_Path = "~/Desktop/RNAPeaks.pdf",   Bed_File_Path = "~/Desktop/BEDFILE_PEAKS.csv",   ... )

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
text: bed data frame containing BED-format peak data. Must least columns chromosome, start, end, strand positions. geneID Gene identifier (gene symbol Ensembl ID starting "ENSG"). gtf Optional pre-loaded GTF annotation data frame. NULL, annotations loaded AnnotationHub based species. species Species annotation lookup. Either "Human" "Mouse". TxID Optional transcript ID plot specific transcript isoform. NA, longest transcript selected. Target_col Column name bed containing protein/target identifiers. omit Character vector target names exclude plot. order_by Method ordering protein tracks: "Count" (default, number peaks), "Target" (alphabetically), "Region" (genomic clustering). order_in Optional character vector specifying exact order targets. merge Minimum gap width (bp) merging nearby peaks. Default 0 (merging). peaks_width Vertical height peak track row. utr_col Color UTR regions. peak_col Color peak rectangles. exon_width Vertical height exon rectangles. utr_width Vertical height UTR rectangles. exon_col Color exon/CDS regions. total_arrows Total number directional arrows drawn across introns indicate transcription direction. Default 6. max_per_intron Maximum number directional arrows drawn per intron. Default 2. five_to_three Logical. TRUE, orients plot 5' left 3' right, regardless strand. negative strand genes, reverses x-axis. Default FALSE (genomic coordinates left--right). RNA_Peaks_File_Path File path save output PDF plot. Bed_File_Path File path save filtered BED data CSV. ... Additional styling arguments passed internal plotting functions. See Styling Parameters section .

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
text: named list containing: plot ggplot2 object peak visualization csv filtered BED data frame used plotting Access result$plot result$csv.

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
text: following parameters can passed via ... customize plot appearance: Gene Structure Colors: exon_fill Fill color exon/CDS regions. Default: "navy" utr_fill Fill color UTR regions. Default: "lightgray" intron_color Color intron lines. Default: "gray60" intron_linewidth Line width introns. Default: 0.9 intron_arrow_len_in Length intron direction arrows inches. Default: 0.15 Peak Styling: peak_alpha Opacity peak rectangles. Default: 0.95 peak_border_color Border color peaks. Default: NA (border) peak_border_linewidth Border line width peaks. Default: 0.4 Background Bands: band_even_fill Fill color even-numbered protein track bands. Default: "#F7F8FA" band_odd_fill Fill color odd-numbered protein track bands. Default: "#FFFFFF" band_sep_color Color band separator lines. Default: "#E5E7EB" band_sep_linewidth Line width band separators. Default: 0.4 Labels: label_size Font size protein labels. Default: 5 label_color Color protein labels. Default: "black" strand_label_size Font size 5'/3' strand labels. Default: 5 strand_label_color Color strand labels. Default: "black" protein_label_x_offset Horizontal offset protein labels bp. Default: 100 Title Axes: title_size Font size plot title. Default: 25 title_color Color plot title. Default: "black" subtitle_size Font size plot subtitle. Default: 12 subtitle_color Color plot subtitle. Default: "black" subtitle_sep Separator gene name coordinates subtitle. Default: ": " axis_title_size Font size axis titles. Default: 11 axis_text_size Font size axis tick labels. Default: 9 Axis Layout: x_lims Custom x-axis limits c(min, max). Default: NULL (auto) axis_pad_bp Padding base pairs added side plot. Default: 500 axis_breaks_n Number axis tick breaks. Default: 5 max_proteins Maximum number protein tracks display. Default: 40 Plot Margins: plot_right_margin Right margin points. Default: 50 plot_top_margin Top margin points. Default: 30 plot_bottom_margin Bottom margin points. Default: 30 plot_left_margin Left margin points. Default: NULL (auto) Highlighted Region: highlighted_region_start Start position region highlight. Default: NULL highlighted_region_stop End position region highlight. Default: NULL highlighted_region_color Color highlighted region. Default: "pink" highlighted_region_opacity Opacity highlighted region. Default: 0.30 Junction Lines: show_junctions Logical. TRUE, draws vertical dashed lines exon/intron boundaries. Default: FALSE junction_color Color junction lines. Default: "gray40" junction_linetype Line type junction lines. Default: "dashed" junction_linewidth Line width junction lines. Default: 0.4 junction_alpha Opacity junction lines. Default: 0.7

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Single Gene — PlotGene
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load GTF annotation (do this once, takes time on first call)   gtf <- LoadGTF(species = "Human")    # Optionally save GTF for future sessions   saveRDS(gtf, "human_gtf.rds")   # Load in future sessions: gtf <- readRDS("human_gtf.rds")    # ----- Using included sample data -----   # sample_bed is included with the package and ready to use   result <- PlotGene(     bed = sample_bed,     geneID = "GAPDH",     gtf = gtf   )    # Access results   result$plot   result$csv    # ----- Using your own BED file -----   # 1. Read your BED file   my_bed <- read.table("my_peaks.bed", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")    # 2. Check Bed file   my_bed <- checkBed(my_bed)    # 3. Plot peaks on gene   result <- PlotGene(     bed = my_bed,     geneID = "BRCA1",     gtf = gtf   )    # Access results   result$plot   result$csv } # }

what: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Genomic Region — PlotRegion
title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Genomic Region — PlotRegion
text: Creates quality visualization RNA-binding protein peaks overlaid multiple gene structures within specified genomic region. Unlike PlotGene(), function can display multiple genes fall within specified coordinates.

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Genomic Region — PlotRegion
code: PlotRegion(   Chr = NULL,   Start = NULL,   End = NULL,   Strand = NULL,   geneID = NULL,   gtf = NULL,   bed = NULL,   species = "Human",   TxID = NA,   Target_col = NULL,   omit = c(),   order_by = "Count",   order_in = NULL,   merge = 0,   peaks_width = 0.3,   utr_col = "dark gray",   peak_col = "Blue",   exon_width = 0.5,   utr_width = 0.3,   exon_col = "black",   total_arrows = 12,   max_per_intron = 5,   five_to_three = FALSE,   RNA_Peaks_File_Path = "~/Desktop/RNAPeaks.pdf",   Bed_File_Path = "~/Desktop/BEDFILE_PEAKS.csv",   ... )

title: Plot RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on a Genomic Region — PlotRegion
text: Chr Chromosome name (e.g., "1", "X", "chr1"). Start Start position genomic region (bp). End End position genomic region (bp). Strand Strand display ("+" "-"). geneID Optional gene identifier focus specific gene. gtf Optional pre-loaded GTF annotation data frame. NULL, annotations loaded AnnotationHub based species. bed data frame containing BED-format peak data. species Species annotation lookup. Either "Human" "Mouse". TxID Optional transcript ID plot specific transcript isoform. Target_col Column name bed containing protein/target identifiers. omit Character vector target names exclude plot. order_by Method ordering protein tracks: "Count" (default), "Target", "Region". order_in Optional character vector specifying exact order targets. merge Minimum gap width (bp) merging nearby peaks. peaks_width Vertical height peak track row. utr_col Color UTR regions. peak_col Color peak rectangles. exon_width Vertical height exon rectangles. utr_width Vertical height UTR rectangles. exon_col Color exon/CDS regions. total_arrows Total number directional arrows drawn across introns indicate transcription direction. Default 12. max_per_intron Maximum number directional arrows drawn per intron. Default 5. five_to_three Logical. TRUE Strand "-", flips x-axis 5' left. Default FALSE. RNA_Peaks_File_Path File path save output PDF plot. Bed_File_Path File path save filtered BED data CSV. ... Additional styling arguments passed internal plotting functions. See Styling Parameters section .

Read your BED file   my_bed <- read.table("my_peaks.bed", header = FALSE, sep = "\t")    # 2. Check Bed file   my_bed <- checkBed(my_bed)    # 3. Plot peaks on region   result <- PlotRegion(     bed = my_bed,     gtf = gtf,     Chr = "12",     Start = 56000000,     End = 56050000,     Strand = "+"   )    # Access results   result$plot   result$csv } # }

what: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: RNAPeaks creates publication-quality visualizations RNA-binding protein (RBP) peaks overlaid gene structures. supports single-gene plots, multi-gene genomic region plots, splicing maps, sequence motif analysis around splice junctions. Integrates GTF annotations Ensembl via AnnotationHub.

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: primary functions generating plots : PlotGene Plot RBP peaks single gene structure. Use want visualize peaks one specific gene. PlotRegion Plot RBP peaks across genomic region containing multiple genes. Use broader regional views. createSplicingMap Analyze protein binding frequency around splice junctions. Shows RBPs bind relative exon/intron boundaries retained vs excluded splicing events. createSequenceMap Analyze sequence motif frequency around splice junctions. Identifies position-specific enrichment motifs different splicing event categories.

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: package includes sample data testing: sample_bed K562 cell line RBP binding peaks, ready use sample_se.mats Sample SE.MATS output splicing analysis

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: typical workflow involves: Use included sample_bed data, load BED file validate checkBed Load GTF annotation LoadGTF store locally (e.g., gtf <- LoadGTF("Human"); save saveRDS(gtf, "gtf.rds") future sessions) Call PlotGene PlotRegion, passing stored gtf object splicing analysis: Prepare SE.MATS output rMATS use sample_se.mats Call createSplicingMap BED SE.MATS data Call createSequenceMap analyze sequence motifs

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: checkBed Validate standardize BED file format LoadGTF Load GTF annotation AnnotationHub. Call store result local variable save disk saveRDS() avoid repeated downloads. Supports "Human" "Mouse".

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
text: Maintainer: Krushna Bhanushali Krushna.Bhanushali@unc.edu Authors: Gilbert Giri Ggiri@unc.edu

title: RNAPeaks: Visualize RNA-Binding Protein Peaks on Gene Structures — RNAPeaks-package
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(RNAPeaks)  # Load GTF once and store locally (do this once per session) gtf <- LoadGTF(species = "Human")  # Optionally save for future sessions to avoid re-downloading saveRDS(gtf, "human_gtf.rds") # In future sessions: gtf <- readRDS("human_gtf.rds")  # ----- Gene-level visualization ----- result <- PlotGene(   bed = sample_bed,   geneID = "GAPDH",   gtf = gtf ) result$plot  # ----- Region-level visualization ----- PlotRegion(   bed = sample_bed,   Chr = "12",   Start = 56000000,   End = 56050000,   Strand = "+",   gtf = gtf )  # ----- Splicing map analysis ----- createSplicingMap(   bed_file = sample_bed,   SEMATS = sample_se.mats )  # ----- Sequence motif analysis ----- library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38) createSequenceMap(   SEMATS = sample_se.mats,   sequence = "YCAY" ) } # }

what: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
text: Validates data frame BED format normalizes column names chromosome identifiers. function expects columns standard BED order: chr, start, end, tag/name, score, strand.

title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
code: checkBed(df)

title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
text: df data frame least 3 columns representing BED data. Columns mapped position: 1=chr, 2=start, 3=end, 4=tag, 5=score, 6=strand. fewer 6 columns provided, missing columns filled defaults: tag="peak", score=0, strand="+".

title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
text: validated data frame normalized column names (chr, start, end, tag, score, strand) chromosome names without "chr" prefix.

title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
text: function performs following: Requires least 3 columns (chr, start, end) Maps columns position canonical BED names Fills missing columns defaults (tag="peak", score=0, strand="+") Validates chromosome character type Verifies end >= start rows Validates strand values "+" "-" Removes "chr" prefix chromosome names Converts chromosome names uppercase

title: Validate and Normalize a BED-Format Data Frame — checkBed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Standard 6-column BED   bed <- read.table("peaks.bed", header = FALSE)   bed <- checkBed(bed)    # 3-column BED (will add defaults for tag, score, strand)   bed3 <- data.frame(chr = "chr1", start = 100, end = 200)   bed3 <- checkBed(bed3) } # }

what: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
text: Analyzes frequency target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency Retained, Excluded, Control splicing events identify position-specific enrichment patterns.

title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
code: createSequenceMap(   SEMATS,   sequence,   genome = NULL,   moving_average = 40,   WidthIntoExon = 50,   WidthIntoIntron = 250,   p_valueRetainedAndExclusion = 0.05,   p_valueControls = 0.95,   retained_IncLevelDifference = 0.1,   exclusion_IncLevelDifference = -0.1,   Min_Count = 50,   groups = c("Retained", "Excluded", "Control"),   control_multiplier = 2,   control_iterations = 20,   cores = 1,   z_threshold = 1.96,   min_consecutive = 10,   one_sided = TRUE,   use_fdr = TRUE,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   show_significance = TRUE,   return_data = FALSE,   return_diagnostics = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   progress_callback = NULL,   title = "",   retained_col = "blue",   excluded_col = "red",   control_col = "black",   line_width = 0.8,   line_alpha = 0.7,   ribbon_alpha = 0.3,   title_size = 20,   title_color = "black",   axis_text_size = 11,   boundary_col = "gray70",   exon_col = "navy",   legend_position = "bottom",   ylab = "Frequency" )

title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
text: SEMATS data frame containing SE.MATS output columns: chr, strand, upstreamES, upstreamEE, exonStart_0base, exonEnd, downstreamES, downstreamEE, GeneID, PValue, FDR, IncLevelDifference, IJC_SAMPLE_1, SJC_SAMPLE_1, IJC_SAMPLE_2, SJC_SAMPLE_2, IncLevel1, IncLevel2 sequence Character string target sequence motif search (e.g., "CCCC", "YGCY"). Supports IUPAC ambiguity codes. genome BSgenome object. Default uses BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38. moving_average Integer specifying window size moving average smoothing. Set NULL 0 disable smoothing. Default 40. WidthIntoExon Integer specifying many bp extend exons. Default 50. WidthIntoIntron Integer specifying many bp extend introns. Default 250. p_valueRetainedAndExclusion P-value threshold retained/excluded events. Default 0.05. p_valueControls P-value threshold control events. Default 0.95. retained_IncLevelDifference Inclusion level difference threshold retained events. Default 0.1. exclusion_IncLevelDifference Inclusion level difference threshold excluded events. Default -0.1. Min_Count Minimum read count threshold. Default 50. groups Character vector specifying event groups process. Options "Retained", "Excluded", /"Control". Default c("Retained", "Excluded", "Control") process groups. Use c("Retained", "Excluded") skip Control group (can large). control_multiplier Numeric multiplier control sample size. number control events sampled per iteration (n_retained + n_excluded) * control_multiplier. Default 1.0. control_iterations Integer number sampling iterations control sampling. final control frequency mean across iterations, standard deviation shown shaded band. Default 20. cores Integer number cores parallel processing. Default 1 (sequential). Set higher faster processing multi-core systems. z_threshold Z-score threshold significance testing. Default 1.96 (corresponds p < 0.05 two-tailed). used use_fdr = FALSE. min_consecutive Minimum number consecutive significant positions required form significant region. Default 10. Helps reduce false positives noise. one_sided Logical. TRUE (default), test enrichment (frequency > control). FALSE, test enrichment depletion. use_fdr Logical. TRUE, use FDR-corrected p-values (Benjamini-Hochberg) significance testing. FALSE (default), use z_threshold directly. fdr_threshold FDR threshold significance use_fdr = TRUE. Default 0.05. show_significance Logical. TRUE (default), displays colored bars plot indicating regions Retained/Excluded differ significantly Control based z-test. return_data Logical. TRUE, returns frequency data frame instead plot. Default FALSE. return_diagnostics Logical. TRUE, returns list containing frequency data, raw bootstrap iteration results (normality testing), significance results. Useful validating bootstrap assumptions. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages. Default TRUE. progress_callback Optional function report progress. Called two arguments: current iteration number total iterations. Used Shiny app progress display. Default NULL (callback). title Character string plot title. Default "" (title). retained_col Color Retained group line. Default "blue". excluded_col Color Excluded group line. Default "red". control_col Color Control group line. Default "black". line_width Numeric line width frequency lines. Default 0.8. line_alpha Numeric alpha (opacity) frequency lines. Default 0.7. ribbon_alpha Numeric alpha SD ribbon around Control. Default 0.3. title_size Numeric font size plot title. Default 20. title_color Color plot title text. Default "black". axis_text_size Numeric font size y-axis tick labels. Default 11. boundary_col Color dashed vertical boundary lines. Default "gray70". exon_col Fill color skipped (middle) exon schematic. Default "navy". legend_position Position legend. Default "bottom". ylab Label y-axis. Default "Frequency".

title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
text: ggplot object showing sequence frequency across 4 regions Retained, Excluded, Control groups. Significant regions (z-test vs Control) shown colored bars plot. Returns data frame return_data = TRUE.

title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
text: function divides splicing event 4 regions (WidthIntoExon + WidthIntoIntron) bp : Region 1: Upstream exon end first intron Region 2: First intron end middle (skipped) exon start Region 3: Middle exon end second intron Region 4: Second intron end downstream exon start Events filtered three groups: Retained: Significant events (PValue < threshold) positive inclusion Excluded: Significant events (PValue < threshold) negative inclusion Control: Non-significant events position, function checks target sequence starts . frequency calculated : (events motif position) / (total events)

title: Analyzes the frequency of a target sequence motif across splicing junction regions. Compares motif frequency between Retained, Excluded, and Control splicing events to identify position-specific enrichment patterns. — createSequenceMap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)  # Basic usage createSequenceMap(SEMATS = sample_se.mats, sequence = "CCCC")  # Search for YCAY motif (Y = C or T) createSequenceMap(SEMATS = sample_se.mats, sequence = "YCAY")  # Return data instead of plot freq_data <- createSequenceMap(SEMATS = sample_se.mats,                                 sequence = "GGGG",                                 return_data = TRUE) } # }

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
text: Analyzes protein binding frequency across splicing junction regions. Uses 4-region structure show protein binding sites appear relative exon/intron boundaries. Filters events Retained, Excluded, Control groups.

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
code: createSplicingMap(   bed_file,   SEMATS,   moving_average = 50,   WidthIntoExon = 50,   WidthIntoIntron = 300,   p_valueRetainedAndExclusion = 0.05,   p_valueControls = 0.95,   retained_IncLevelDifference = 0.1,   exclusion_IncLevelDifference = -0.1,   Min_Count = 50,   groups = c("Retained", "Excluded", "Control"),   control_multiplier = 2,   control_iterations = 20,   cores = 1,   z_threshold = 1.96,   min_consecutive = 10,   one_sided = TRUE,   use_fdr = TRUE,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   show_significance = TRUE,   return_data = FALSE,   return_diagnostics = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   progress_callback = NULL,   title = "",   retained_col = "blue",   excluded_col = "red",   control_col = "black",   line_width = 0.8,   line_alpha = 0.7,   ribbon_alpha = 0.3,   title_size = 20,   title_color = "black",   axis_text_size = 11,   boundary_col = "gray70",   exon_col = "navy",   legend_position = "bottom",   ylab = "Frequency" )

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
text: bed_file Either file path BED file data frame containing BED data columns: chr, start, end, tag, score, strand SEMATS data frame containing SE.MATS output columns: chr, strand, upstreamES, upstreamEE, exonStart_0base, exonEnd, downstreamES, downstreamEE, GeneID, PValue, FDR, IncLevelDifference, IJC_SAMPLE_1, SJC_SAMPLE_1, IJC_SAMPLE_2, SJC_SAMPLE_2, IncLevel1, IncLevel2 moving_average Integer specifying window size moving average smoothing. Set NULL 0 disable smoothing. Default 50. WidthIntoExon Integer specifying many bp extend exons. Default 50. WidthIntoIntron Integer specifying many bp extend introns. Default 300. p_valueRetainedAndExclusion P-value threshold retained/excluded events. Default 0.05. p_valueControls P-value threshold control events. Default 0.95. retained_IncLevelDifference Inclusion level difference threshold retained events. Default 0.1. exclusion_IncLevelDifference Inclusion level difference threshold excluded events. Default -0.1. Min_Count Minimum read count threshold. Default 50. groups Character vector specifying event groups process. Options "Retained", "Excluded", /"Control". Default c("Retained", "Excluded", "Control") process groups. Use c("Retained", "Excluded") skip Control group (can large). control_multiplier Numeric multiplier control sample size. number control events sampled per iteration (n_retained + n_excluded) * control_multiplier. Default 2.0. control_iterations Integer number sampling iterations control sampling. final control frequency mean across iterations, standard deviation shown shaded band. Default 20. cores Number cores parallel processing. Default 1 (sequential). z_threshold Z-score threshold significance testing. Default 1.96 (corresponds p < 0.05 two-tailed). used use_fdr = FALSE. min_consecutive Minimum number consecutive significant positions required form significant region. Default 10. Helps reduce false positives noise. one_sided Logical. TRUE (default), test enrichment (frequency > control). FALSE, test enrichment depletion. use_fdr Logical. TRUE, use FDR-corrected p-values (Benjamini-Hochberg) significance testing. FALSE (default), use z_threshold directly. fdr_threshold FDR threshold significance use_fdr = TRUE. Default 0.05. show_significance Logical. TRUE (default), displays colored bars plot indicating regions Retained/Excluded differ significantly Control based z-test. return_data Logical. TRUE, returns frequency data frame instead plot. Default FALSE. return_diagnostics Logical. TRUE, returns list containing frequency data, raw bootstrap iteration results (normality testing), significance results. Useful validating bootstrap assumptions. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. TRUE, prints progress messages. Default TRUE. progress_callback Optional function report progress. Called two arguments: current iteration number total iterations. Used Shiny app progress display. Default NULL (callback). title Character string plot title. Default "" (title). retained_col Color Retained group line. Default "blue". excluded_col Color Excluded group line. Default "red". control_col Color Control group line. Default "black". line_width Numeric line width frequency lines. Default 0.8. line_alpha Numeric alpha (opacity) frequency lines. Default 0.7. ribbon_alpha Numeric alpha SD ribbon around Control. Default 0.3. title_size Numeric font size plot title. Default 20. title_color Color plot title text. Default "black". axis_text_size Numeric font size y-axis tick labels. Default 11. boundary_col Color dashed vertical boundary lines. Default "gray70". exon_col Fill color skipped (middle) exon schematic. Default "navy". legend_position Position legend. Default "bottom". ylab Label y-axis. Default "Frequency".

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
text: ggplot object showing protein binding frequency across 4 regions Retained, Excluded, Control groups. Significant regions (z-test vs Control) shown colored bars plot. Returns data frame return_data = TRUE.

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
text: function divides splicing event 4 regions (WidthIntoExon + WidthIntoIntron) bp : Region 1 (UE-UI5): Upstream exon end first intron Region 2 (UI3-EX3): First intron end middle (skipped) exon start Region 3 (EX5-DI5): Middle exon end second intron Region 4 (DI3-DE): Second intron end downstream exon start Events filtered three groups: Retained: Significant events (PValue < threshold) negative IncLevelDifference Excluded: Significant events (PValue < threshold) positive IncLevelDifference Control: Non-significant events stable inclusion levels

title: Create Splicing Map — createSplicingMap
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load BED file and SE.MATS data bed <- read.table("peaks.bed") semats <- read.table("SE.MATS.JC.txt", header = TRUE)  # Basic usage createSplicingMap(bed_file = bed, SEMATS = semats)  # Use parallel processing createSplicingMap(bed_file = bed, SEMATS = semats, cores = 4)  # Return data instead of plot freq_data <- createSplicingMap(bed_file = bed,                                 SEMATS = semats,                                 return_data = TRUE) } # }

title: Human GTF Gene Annotation — gtf_human
text: Pre-loaded Ensembl GTF annotation human genes (GRCh38). bundled dataset eliminates need download AnnotationHub, enabling offline use faster loading.

title: Human GTF Gene Annotation — gtf_human
code: gtf_human

title: Human GTF Gene Annotation — gtf_human
text: data frame containing GTF annotation following columns: seqnames Chromosome start Feature start position end Feature end position width Feature width bp strand Strand (+ -) source Annotation source type Feature type (gene, transcript, exon, CDS, UTR, etc.) score Annotation score phase CDS phase (0, 1, 2) gene_id Ensembl gene ID gene_version Ensembl gene version gene_name Gene symbol gene_source Gene annotation source gene_biotype Gene biotype (protein_coding, lncRNA, etc.) transcript_id Ensembl transcript ID transcript_version Ensembl transcript version transcript_name Transcript name transcript_source Transcript annotation source transcript_biotype Transcript biotype transcript_support_level Transcript support level (TSL) exon_number Exon number within transcript exon_id Ensembl exon ID exon_version Ensembl exon version protein_id Ensembl protein ID protein_version Ensembl protein version ccds_id CCDS identifier tag Feature tag (e.g., basic, Ensembl_canonical)

title: Human GTF Gene Annotation — gtf_human
text: Ensembl via AnnotationHub (AH110867)

title: Human GTF Gene Annotation — gtf_human
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Load the bundled GTF   data(gtf_human)    # Use with PlotGene (no download required)   data(sample_bed)   PlotGene(bed = sample_bed, geneID = "GAPDH", gtf = gtf_human) } # }

what: Sample SE.MATS Skipped-Exon Splicing Events — sample_se.mats
title: Sample SE.MATS Skipped-Exon Splicing Events — sample_se.mats
text: dataset skipped exon (SE) alternative splicing events generated rMATS, provided testing demonstration splicing map sequence motif analysis functions RNAPeaks.

title: Sample SE.MATS Skipped-Exon Splicing Events — sample_se.mats
code: sample_se.mats

title: Sample SE.MATS Skipped-Exon Splicing Events — sample_se.mats
text: data frame 87,736 observations 23 variables: ID Event ID GeneID Ensembl gene ID geneSymbol Gene symbol chr Chromosome strand Strand (+ -) exonStart_0base Skipped exon start position (0-based) exonEnd Skipped exon end position upstreamES Upstream exon start position upstreamEE Upstream exon end position downstreamES Downstream exon start position downstreamEE Downstream exon end position ID.1 Duplicate ID column IJC_SAMPLE_1 Inclusion junction counts sample 1 SJC_SAMPLE_1 Skipping junction counts sample 1 IJC_SAMPLE_2 Inclusion junction counts sample 2 SJC_SAMPLE_2 Skipping junction counts sample 2 IncFormLen Inclusion form length SkipFormLen Skipping form length PValue P-value differential splicing FDR False discovery rate adjusted p-value IncLevel1 Inclusion levels sample 1 IncLevel2 Inclusion levels sample 2 IncLevelDifference Difference inclusion levels samples (positive = inclusion sample 1; negative = skipping)

title: Sample SE.MATS Skipped-Exon Splicing Events — sample_se.mats
